title: The Warrior Who Trembled
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: My bow slips.
My mouth is dry.
The men I must face are my teachers, my elders, my blood.
What victory asks this price?
What kingdom is worth a house on fire?
I would rather lay down my arms
than rise through a river of my own people.
stanzas: My bow slips.
My mouth is dry.
The men I must face are my teachers, my elders, my blood.
What victory asks this price?
What kingdom is worth a house on fire?
I would rather lay down my arms
than rise through a river of my own people.
excerpt: My bow slips. My mouth is dry.

title: Stand Up and See Clearly
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: This collapse is not your truth.
It is a passing storm over a steady sky.
Stand.
Cut through this softness.
A warrior does not worship confusion.
stanzas: This collapse is not your truth.
It is a passing storm over a steady sky.
Stand.
Cut through this softness.
A warrior does not worship confusion.
excerpt: This collapse is not your truth. It is a passing storm over a steady sky.

title: No Blade Reaches the Self
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Weapons do not cut it.
Fire does not burn it.
Water does not drown it.
Wind does not dry it.
The Self is unborn, undying,
older than time,
still when worlds break.
stanzas: Weapons do not cut it.
Fire does not burn it.
Water does not drown it.
Wind does not dry it.
The Self is unborn, undying,
older than time,
still when worlds break.
excerpt: Weapons do not cut it. Fire does not burn it.

title: Bodies Fall, Being Remains
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: As a person changes worn-out clothes,
Being puts down one body
and takes up another.
Do not mistake the garment for the wearer.
Do not mistake endings for extinction.
stanzas: As a person changes worn-out clothes,
Being puts down one body
and takes up another.
Do not mistake the garment for the wearer.
Do not mistake endings for extinction.
excerpt: As a person changes worn-out clothes, Being puts down one body

title: Do the Work, Release the Claim
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: You have authority over action,
never over the harvest.
Do not work for reward.
Do not fall into inaction.
Move with full force,
free from the hunger to possess outcomes.
stanzas: You have authority over action,
never over the harvest.
Do not work for reward.
Do not fall into inaction.
Move with full force,
free from the hunger to possess outcomes.
excerpt: You have authority over action, never over the harvest.

title: Skill in Action
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Stay even in gain and loss,
in praise and blame,
in victory and defeat.
This balance is yoga—
a fierce precision in living,
a mastery of action without inner fracture.
stanzas: Stay even in gain and loss,
in praise and blame,
in victory and defeat.
This balance is yoga—
a fierce precision in living,
a mastery of action without inner fracture.
excerpt: Stay even in gain and loss, in praise and blame,

title: The Chain of Ruin
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: You dwell on objects.
Attachment grows.
Attachment turns to craving.
Craving turns to anger.
Anger clouds memory,
memory breaks judgment,
judgment collapses,
and a life goes down in flames.
stanzas: You dwell on objects.
Attachment grows.
Attachment turns to craving.
Craving turns to anger.
Anger clouds memory,
memory breaks judgment,
judgment collapses,
and a life goes down in flames.
excerpt: You dwell on objects. Attachment grows.

title: The Sea-Mind
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Rivers pour into the sea.
The sea is full yet unmoved.
So is the one at peace:
desires arrive,
desires pass,
but the center does not flood.
stanzas: Rivers pour into the sea.
The sea is full yet unmoved.
So is the one at peace:
desires arrive,
desires pass,
but the center does not flood.
excerpt: Rivers pour into the sea. The sea is full yet unmoved.

title: Action Is Better Than Escape
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: No one lives even a moment without action.
Nature moves all.
Do what is yours to do.
Right action is cleaner than frozen idealism.
stanzas: No one lives even a moment without action.
Nature moves all.
Do what is yours to do.
Right action is cleaner than frozen idealism.
excerpt: No one lives even a moment without action. Nature moves all.

title: Offer Every Act
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Cook, fight, serve, study, build—
make each act an offering.
When action is given upward,
it stops chaining the heart
to fear and pride.
stanzas: Cook, fight, serve, study, build—
make each act an offering.
When action is given upward,
it stops chaining the heart
to fear and pride.
excerpt: Cook, fight, serve, study, build— make each act an offering.

title: Whenever Justice Fades
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: When truth thins,
when cruelty rises,
when the weak are crushed,
I come.
Age after age,
I protect what is living,
I break what is poisonous,
I reset the moral axis.
stanzas: When truth thins,
when cruelty rises,
when the weak are crushed,
I come.
Age after age,
I protect what is living,
I break what is poisonous,
I reset the moral axis.
excerpt: When truth thins, when cruelty rises,

title: Fire of Knowledge
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: As fire reduces wood to ash,
clear knowledge burns old karma.
Take up that fire.
Let it pass through doubt,
fear,
and inherited darkness.
stanzas: As fire reduces wood to ash,
clear knowledge burns old karma.
Take up that fire.
Let it pass through doubt,
fear,
and inherited darkness.
excerpt: As fire reduces wood to ash, clear knowledge burns old karma.

title: The True Renunciate
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Not the one who quits work,
but the one who quits craving
is the true renunciate.
Hands active.
Heart unbound.
That is freedom in motion.
stanzas: Not the one who quits work,
but the one who quits craving
is the true renunciate.
Hands active.
Heart unbound.
That is freedom in motion.
excerpt: Not the one who quits work, but the one who quits craving

title: Lift Yourself
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Lift yourself by yourself.
Do not let yourself fall.
Your mind can be your ally,
or your sabotage.
Train it,
and it becomes a clear blade.
stanzas: Lift yourself by yourself.
Do not let yourself fall.
Your mind can be your ally,
or your sabotage.
Train it,
and it becomes a clear blade.
excerpt: Lift yourself by yourself. Do not let yourself fall.

title: The Lamp in Windless Air
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: In stillness,
the disciplined mind is a lamp in a windless place.
No flicker.
No scatter.
Just steady seeing.
stanzas: In stillness,
the disciplined mind is a lamp in a windless place.
No flicker.
No scatter.
Just steady seeing.
excerpt: In stillness, the disciplined mind is a lamp in a windless place.

title: Nothing Is Lost
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: No effort on this path is wasted.
No sincere move is erased.
Even a little of this practice
rescues a life
from immense fear.
stanzas: No effort on this path is wasted.
No sincere move is erased.
Even a little of this practice
rescues a life
from immense fear.
excerpt: No effort on this path is wasted. No sincere move is erased.

title: I Am the Taste of Water
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: I am the taste in water,
the light in sun and moon,
the syllable in sacred sound,
the courage in the brave.
Wherever life shines,
I am that shine.
stanzas: I am the taste in water,
the light in sun and moon,
the syllable in sacred sound,
the courage in the brave.
Wherever life shines,
I am that shine.
excerpt: I am the taste in water, the light in sun and moon,

title: Remember Me at the Last Breath
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: What you remember at death,
that you move toward.
Remember Me now,
in work,
in battle,
in breath—
then the final moment will not betray you.
stanzas: What you remember at death,
that you move toward.
Remember Me now,
in work,
in battle,
in breath—
then the final moment will not betray you.
excerpt: What you remember at death, that you move toward.

title: A Leaf, A Flower, A Heart
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: A leaf,
a flower,
a fruit,
a little water—
if offered with love,
I receive it.
I look at the heart,
not the size of the gift.
stanzas: A leaf,
a flower,
a fruit,
a little water—
if offered with love,
I receive it.
I look at the heart,
not the size of the gift.
excerpt: A leaf, a flower,

title: Take Refuge and Rise
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Drop all lesser duties.
Come to Me alone for refuge.
Do not fear.
I will free you
from the burden of all past wrong.
Now rise.
stanzas: Drop all lesser duties.
Come to Me alone for refuge.
Do not fear.
I will free you
from the burden of all past wrong.
Now rise.
excerpt: Drop all lesser duties. Come to Me alone for refuge.

title: The Discipline of Steady Wisdom
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: You ask how the one of steady wisdom lives.
Listen.
They are not dragged by pleasure,
not shattered by pain.
They do not chase what glitters,
and they do not collapse when it breaks.
They gather the senses
the way a tortoise draws in its limbs.
Not from fear,
but from sovereign control.
Their joy is not rented from the world.
It rises from within,
self-lit,
quiet,
unstealable.
When desire is denied by force,
it waits in the dark.
When truth is seen directly,
desire loses command.
Then anger loosens,
memory clears,
intelligence returns to its throne.
Night for the crowd
is waking for the sage.
Where others run after noise,
the sage watches.
Where others sleep through reality,
the sage stands alert.
Be like the ocean:
rivers enter,
storms strike,
its depth remains.
This is the peace that does not bargain.
This is the victory no battle can reverse.

title: The Law of Action and Offering
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: No one escapes action.
Breath acts.
Thought acts.
Silence acts.
Even refusal is a move in the field.
So stop worshiping the fantasy of neutrality.
Do the work born of your nature.
Do it well.
Do it without ownership.
When action is done as sacrifice,
it purifies.
When done for ego,
it binds.
The world turns through exchange:
life feeding life,
duty feeding order,
offering feeding grace.
Break this wheel,
and society rots in appetite.
Keep it turning,
and even ordinary labor becomes sacred.
Set the standard by your life.
People follow what the strong normalize.
If you stand clear,
clarity spreads.
If you perform chaos,
chaos multiplies.
Therefore act—
not to inflate your name,
but to uphold the world.
Work with full intensity,
empty of possessiveness,
steady in success and failure.
That is freedom inside responsibility.

Breath acts.
Thought acts.
Silence acts.
Even refusal is a move in the field.

Work with full intensity,
empty of possessiveness,
steady in success and failure.
That is freedom inside responsibility.
excerpt: No one escapes action. Breath acts.

title: Knowledge, Work, and the Inner Fire
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: I taught this ancient yoga
at the dawn of kings,
then it was worn down by time.
Now I speak it again,
because you can carry it in battle.
You ask how the timeless can be born.
I take form by will,
not compulsion.
When justice declines,
I enter history—
to protect,
to cut through corruption,
to re-anchor dharma.
See action in inaction,
inaction in action.
This is subtle intelligence.
The wise move without the fever of "I am the doer."
Their work burns clean,
because ego is not fueling the fire.
Many offerings exist:
study,
austerity,
service,
breath,
self-control.
But knowledge-offering is highest,
because it turns the whole mind into flame.
Even if you were the worst among sinners,
this knowledge is a boat across the flood.
As blazing fire turns wood to ash,
clear insight turns karma to ash.
So cut doubt with the sword of understanding.
Stand up.
Take your place.
Move.

at the dawn of kings,

because you can carry it in battle.

title: Renunciation Without Withdrawal
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: You think renunciation means quitting work.
No.
Real renunciation is quitting clinging.
Hands can remain fully engaged
while the heart stays free.
The immature divide paths:
"knowledge" here,
"action" there.
The mature see one summit
reached by different trails.
Work done without attachment
arrives at the same freedom
as disciplined insight.
Offer outcomes.
Drop the claim,
"This is mine, I made this, I own this."
Then action does not stain.
Like a lotus leaf in water,
you touch the world
without being soaked by it.
The one integrated in yoga
acts for purification,
not for self-advertisement.
They eat, walk, speak, choose,
but inwardly know:
"Nature moves through its qualities.
I am not the chain of compulsions."
Peace comes to the one
who releases the fruit.
Restlessness haunts the one
who grips result after result.
So act in the city,
in the marketplace,
in the battlefield,
and remain inwardly vast.
This is liberation
in the middle of life.

No.

title: The Architecture of Meditation
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Choose a clean place.
Seat yourself steadily.
Body, head, and neck aligned.
Gaze soft,
breath even,
mind gathered.
Do not overeat.
Do not starve.
Do not oversleep.
Do not grind yourself to dust.
Yoga matures in balance,
not extremism.
Again and again,
when the mind runs,
bring it back.
Not with violence,
with authority.
Not once,
a thousand times.
That repetition is the training.
When discipline deepens,
mind settles like a flame
in windless air.
From that stillness,
a joy appears
that does not depend on objects.
A direct contact with the Self,
intimate,
unshaken.
Then even heavy sorrow
cannot uproot you.
This is the gain
beyond all gains.
The restless mind is hard to master,
yes.
But through practice
and non-attachment,
it becomes a loyal instrument.
One who sees the Self in all beings,
and all beings in the Self,
walks through conflict
without hatred.
That one is never separate from Me.

Seat yourself steadily.
Body, head, and neck aligned.
Gaze soft,
breath even,
mind gathered.
Do not overeat.
Do not starve.
Do not oversleep.
Do not grind yourself to dust.

title: Knowing the Vast and the Near
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: I will speak complete knowledge:
what is seen,
and what sees.
Matter and consciousness,
field and knower of the field.
Know both,
and confusion starts to dissolve.
Earth, water, fire, air, space,
mind, intellect, and ego—
this is My lower nature.
Beyond it is the higher:
the living force
that sustains worlds.
Everything rests on Me
like pearls on a thread.
I am the taste in water,
the brilliance in sunlight,
the syllable in sacred sound,
the strength free of greed.
Three qualities weave experience:
clarity,
restless passion,
inertia.
All arise in Me,
yet I am beyond them.
The world, enchanted by these modes,
does not recognize the source.
Rare is the one
who breaks through this enchantment
and turns toward the Real.
Some seek Me in distress,
some for gain,
some for knowledge,
some in pure devotion.
All are noble,
but the one who loves with whole being
lives in Me like breath in life.
After many births,
the wise arrive at one knowing:
all this is the One.
Such a soul is exceedingly rare.

title: The Two Paths at Death
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: What you remember as you leave the body,
that current carries you.
So train remembrance now,
inside action,
inside struggle,
inside ordinary hours.
Fix the mind on the Supreme,
steady the life-force,
and depart through focused awareness.
Not by panic,
not by chance,
by prepared consciousness.
All worlds, even heaven-worlds,
turn in cycles of appearing and dissolving.
Do not settle for temporary crowns.
Reach that state beyond return,
where becoming ends.
There are paths of departure:
one through light,
one through dim return.
The yogi who knows this
is not confused by timing
or celestial mechanics.
Therefore at all times,
practice union.
Remember,
act,
offer.
Then the final threshold
is not an accident,
but fulfillment.

that current carries you.

inside action,

title: The Royal Secret
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: This is the kingly knowledge,
the kingly secret:
clear,
transforming,
possible in daily life,
and aligned with the deepest good.
I pervade the universe,
yet I remain unbound.
Beings rest in Me,
but I am not trapped in them.
See the paradox and be free:
intimacy without imprisonment.
People miss Me
because I appear in human form.
They see the mask,
not the blazing source behind it.
But great souls recognize,
and they love without distraction.
Even those who worship other powers
with sincere faith
are reaching toward Me,
though they may not know the full arc.
Still,
worship aimed at fragments
yields fragmentary results.
Aim at the Whole.
Whatever you do,
whatever you eat,
whatever you offer,
whatever you endure—
do it as an offering to Me.
Then action no longer binds.
No one is disqualified.
Even one with a damaged past,
if wholly turned in devotion,
walks quickly into dharma.
My devotee never perishes.
Let this be known.

title: The Splendors of the Divine
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: You ask how to remember Me in all things.
See Me in what is most luminous,
most powerful,
most essential.
Among lights,
I am the sun.
Among stars,
the moon.
Among scriptures,
the song of wisdom.
Among senses,
the mind that coordinates all.
Among beings,
consciousness itself.
Among mountains,
I am the Himalaya.
Among rivers,
the Ganga.
Among trees,
the sacred ashvattha.
Among warriors,
I am righteous courage.
Among rulers,
just authority.
I am time that ripens events.
I am the beginning,
the middle,
and the end of every process.
I am memory,
intelligence,
patience,
and the force that renews worlds.
Wherever you see beauty with strength,
excellence with grace,
order with brilliance—
know that as a spark of My vastness.
Yet all these splendors
are only a hint.
With a single fraction of Myself,
I sustain the universe.

title: Vision of the Cosmic Form
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: You asked to see Me as I truly am.
No ordinary eyes can hold this vision.
I grant divine sight—look.
Countless faces,
countless eyes,
countless wonders,
weapons lifted,
garlands blazing,
light without boundary.
If a thousand suns rose at once,
that might resemble a fragment
of this radiance.
In that body:
all gods,
all beings,
all worlds,
all directions.
No beginning.
No middle.
No end.
Only immeasurable force.
I see warriors rushing into Your mouths
as rivers rush to sea,
as moths rush into flame.
Teeth of fire.
Skulls breaking.
Time devouring names.
Who are You in this terrifying majesty?
I am Time,
world-destroying,
risen to consume.
Even without you,
these armies fall.
So rise.
Win glory.
Be My instrument.
Those already struck by destiny
will be struck through your action.
Shaken,
I bow.
Take again the gentle form,
friend and guide,
that my heart can bear.
By devotion alone
this form is truly known.
stanzas: You asked to see Me as I truly am.
No ordinary eyes can hold this vision.
I grant divine sight—look.
Countless faces,
countless eyes,
countless wonders,
weapons lifted,

title: The Way of Love and Steadiness
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Those who fix their minds on Me
with trust and constancy
are deeply united.
Those who seek the unmanifest absolute
also reach,
but their path is harder
for embodied minds.
So place your mind in Me.
If you cannot,
practice remembrance.
If practice falters,
work for Me.
If that is hard,
renounce the fruits of your work.
Step by step,
peace grows.
What kind of devotee is dearest?
One who harms none,
who is friendly,
free from possessiveness,
steady in joy and grief,
forgiving,
disciplined,
clear,
and inwardly anchored.
One who does not disturb the world,
and is not disturbed by it.
One free from agitation,
fear,
and explosive reaction.
One who is pure,
skillful,
quietly effective,
and unattached to status.
One equal in honor and insult,
in heat and cold,
in praise and blame,
content with what comes,
with mind dedicated,
with faith alive.
Such a person walks the nectar-path.
Such a heart is exceedingly dear to Me.

title: Field and Knower
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: This body is the field.
The one aware of it is the knower.
Understand both,
and understanding matures.
What is called knowledge?
Humility,
non-violence,
straightforwardness,
self-mastery,
endurance,
detachment from ego,
seeing birth, aging, illness, and death clearly,
not clinging,
steady devotion to truth,
love of solitude,
constancy in self-inquiry.
Everything contrary to this
is ignorance.
What is to be known?
The beginningless Reality:
not merely being,
not merely non-being,
beyond categories.
Hands and feet everywhere,
eyes and faces everywhere,
hearing everywhere,
pervading all.
It shines through every sense
while itself beyond the senses.
Unattached,
yet sustaining all.
Inside and outside all beings.
Near,
yet seemingly far.
Undivided,
yet appearing divided.
Know nature and spirit:
nature generates cause and effect,
spirit experiences.
Attachment to qualities
binds spirit to repeated birth.
One who sees
the same imperishable Self
in all perishing forms
truly sees.
Seeing this,
one does not wound the Self by the self.
One reaches the highest.

title: Beyond the Three Qualities
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Nature moves through three strands:
clarity,
passion,
and inertia.
These qualities bind the deathless self
to body and behavior.
Clarity illumines,
but can bind through attachment to happiness and knowledge.
Passion drives action,
but binds through craving and unrest.
Inertia clouds,
slows,
and binds through negligence and sleep.
When clarity dominates,
understanding shines.
When passion dominates,
greed and agitation rise.
When inertia dominates,
confusion thickens.
At death,
these tendencies shape direction.
But the one who sees,
"qualities are acting on qualities,"
steps back from false identification.
Not hating when modes arise,
not craving when they pass,
stable,
self-settled,
same in pleasure and pain,
same in mud, stone, and gold,
same in honor and insult,
beyond compulsive reaction—
that one crosses the qualities.
How to cross fully?
Unswerving devotion to Me.
Then one is fit for the Infinite,
for immortal peace,
for the happiness that does not end.

for immortal peace,
for the happiness that does not end.

title: The Inverted Tree
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: There is a cosmic tree
with roots above
and branches below,
fed by the qualities,
its leaves the hymns of wisdom.
To know this tree
is to know the structure of becoming.
Its branches spread through the worlds,
its shoots are sense-objects,
its roots sink downward
as karmic habits in human life.
Its form is hard to grasp here:
no clear beginning,
no clear end,
no fixed ground.
Cut this deep-rooted tree
with the strong axe of non-attachment.
Then seek the supreme state
from which none return.
Take refuge in the primal Source
from whom this ancient flow began.
Free from pride and delusion,
victorious over attachment,
steadfast in the inner Self,
desires calmed,
beyond the swing of opposites—
one reaches that imperishable goal.
There,
neither sun nor moon nor fire is needed.
That is My supreme abode.
A fragment of Me,
becoming the individual soul,
draws mind and senses into nature.
As wind carries fragrance,
the soul carries impressions
from body to body.
The unwise do not see this movement.
The wise do.
Remember the source.
Return.

title: Two Natures in One World
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Human beings display two broad patterns:
divine orientation
and destructive orientation.
The divine qualities free.
The destructive qualities bind.
Fearlessness,
purity,
discipline,
generosity,
truthfulness,
self-control,
compassion,
steadiness,
forgiveness,
humility—
these build a luminous life.
Hypocrisy,
arrogance,
self-importance,
anger,
harshness,
and ignorance
pull consciousness downward.
Such people say:
"The world has no truth, no moral base,
only appetite and accident."
From that view,
insatiable desire grows.
Driven by endless craving,
trapped in nets of delusion,
they seek wealth by unjust means,
thinking:
"I gained this today.
I will gain more tomorrow.
I defeated that enemy.
I am powerful,
I am happy."
Thus they spiral into darker states.
Three gates lead to ruin:
craving,
anger,
greed.
Abandon all three.
Then one acts for the highest good,
and rises.
Let wisdom guide action.
Do not make impulse your scripture.
With right discernment,
walk toward freedom.

title: Faith Shapes the Person
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: A person is made of faith.
As one's faith,
so one becomes.
Faith also carries the color
of the three qualities.
Clarity-driven faith seeks what uplifts,
with discipline and balance.
Passion-driven faith seeks display,
power,
and result.
Inertia-driven faith seeks distortion,
cruelty,
and confusion.
Food reflects this too:
clarity prefers what nourishes and steadies,
passion chases the overstimulating,
inertia sinks into stale and toxic.
Austerity can be of body,
speech,
and mind:
respect,
cleanliness,
non-harm,
truth spoken kindly,
study,
inner serenity,
self-restraint,
purity of intent.
Done with faith and without vanity,
it clarifies life.
Done for show,
it corrodes.
Done through delusion,
it harms self and others.
Giving also has three forms:
giving at the right time and place,
to one in need,
without expectation—this is clear.
Giving for return or recognition—this is restless.
Giving carelessly,
with contempt,
without discernment—this is dark.
Let your actions be anchored in the Real.
Let intention be clean.
Then faith becomes a force
for liberation.

title: Renunciation and Final Freedom
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: You ask again about renunciation.
Hear the final clarity.
Renouncing selfish fruits
is wise.
Abandoning rightful action
out of confusion
is not.
Action cannot be fully avoided
while embodied.
What can be relinquished
is possessiveness toward results.
That renunciation brings peace.
Knowledge,
action,
doer,
intellect,
resolve,
and happiness—
all appear in three modes.
Discern these differences,
then choose upward.
Better one's own duty,
though imperfect,
than another's duty done brilliantly.
Work aligned with your nature
purifies over time.
Every path has some smoke,
as fire does.
Do not quit because conditions are mixed.
With mind purified,
senses mastered,
desire and ego dropped,
living lightly,
devoted to meditation,
one becomes fit for union with Brahman.
In that state,
no grief,
no craving,
equal vision toward all.
From that equality,
true devotion flowers.
Fix your mind on Me.
Be devoted.
Offer.
Bow.
You will come to Me.
This is My promise,
because you are dear.
Now the most secret teaching:
leave all lesser refuges.
Take refuge in Me alone.
I will free you from all bondage.
Do not fear.
Deliberate fully.
Then act as you choose.
My confusion is gone,
Arjuna says.
Memory restored.
I stand ready.
I will act.

title: The Burden of Compassion
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: My limbs fail,
my skin burns,
my mind reels.
Bad omens crowd the heart.
I do not desire victory,
kingdom,
or pleasures
bought with family blood.
What joy remains
if those for whom joy is sought
fall by my hand?
Teacher,
uncle,
grandfather,
friend—
how can I strike them,
even for dominion over three worlds?
Sin will stain us
if we kill our own.
They are blinded by greed.
Must we also be blind?
Seeing clearly,
should we not turn away?
If clan-law dies,
order collapses,
memory decays,
and generations suffer.
Better to be killed unarmed
than to live with that ruin.
Saying this,
I sank into the chariot,
bow slipping from my hand,
heart split between duty and love.

title: From Collapse to Clarity
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: You grieve for those
who need no grieving.
The wise do not mourn
for living or dead.
There was never a time
when you,
I,
or these rulers did not exist,
nor will there be a time
when we cease to be.
Childhood,
youth,
and age pass through one body;
so does the passage to another.
The steady are not confused by this.
Heat and cold,
pleasure and pain,
arrive and depart.
Endure them.
The unreal has no lasting being.
The Real never stops being.
Know this distinction
and stand in it.
The embodied Self is deathless,
immeasurable,
indestructible.
Bodies end.
Therefore fight.
Who thinks "I kill"
or "I am killed"
does not yet see.
Unborn,
eternal,
ancient,
this Self is not slain
when the body falls.
As old clothes are discarded,
new garments are taken.
So with embodiment.
No weapon cuts it.
No fire burns it.
No water wets it.
No wind dries it.
It is stable,
all-pervading,
immovable,
ever the same.
If you still believe birth and death are absolute,
even then grief is misplaced:
what is born will die,
what dies will be born.
Therefore rise.
Fulfill your duty with a clear heart.

title: The Ethics of Equanimity
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Consider your own calling:
for a warrior,
no good is higher
than a just battle.
To refuse this moment
is to abandon duty and honor.
People will speak of your disgrace
for generations.
To one once honored,
disgrace cuts deeper than death.
If slain,
you gain heaven.
If victorious,
you uphold the earth.
Therefore stand,
resolved to fight.
Hold pleasure and pain alike,
gain and loss alike,
victory and defeat alike.
Then act.
Action from this balance
does not wound the soul.
On this path,
no effort is wasted,
no reverse is final.
Even a small practice
protects from immense fear.
The steady mind is one-pointed.
The scattered mind
chases countless branches:
status,
ritual display,
heavenly reward,
endless desires dressed as virtue.
Rise beyond that noise.
When your intelligence crosses
the dense forest of confusion,
you will become indifferent
to what has been heard
and what is yet to be heard.
When mind stands unmoving
in deep stillness,
you arrive in yoga.

title: How the Steady One Walks
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: When one drops all cravings
born of restless imagination,
and is satisfied in the Self alone,
that one is called steady in wisdom.
Unshaken in sorrow,
free from thirst in pleasure,
released from attachment,
fear,
and anger—
that sage is anchored.
Withdrawing senses
the way a tortoise withdraws limbs,
one protects attention.
Objects may recede,
but taste remains.
When the Supreme is seen,
even that taste dissolves.
The senses are turbulent.
They can drag away
even a striving mind.
So gather them,
center in Me,
and let understanding stand guard.
From fixation comes attachment,
from attachment desire,
from desire frustration,
from frustration anger,
from anger delusion,
from delusion memory-loss,
from memory-loss ruin of intelligence,
from ruined intelligence, collapse.
But one who moves among objects
with senses disciplined,
free from attraction and aversion,
finds peace.
In peace,
all sorrow thins,
and understanding stabilizes quickly.
For the undisciplined,
there is no wisdom,
no contemplation,
no peace.
Without peace,
how can there be joy?
So remain vigilant.
When the world sleeps to truth,
be awake.

be awake.

title: Why the Wise Still Act
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Two orientations were taught:
knowledge for contemplative temperaments,
action-yoga for those called to engaged life.
But understand:
no one reaches freedom
by refusing action.
Nature compels movement.
One who restrains the body
but mentally indulges desires
is self-deceived.
Better to act with disciplined senses,
without attachment,
in service of what must be done.
Action is superior to inertia.
Even bodily maintenance
requires action.
Therefore perform your duty
as offering,
not as private consumption.
Action done for sacrifice
frees.
Action done for ego binds.
At creation,
life was yoked to sacrifice:
"Through this, prosper.
Let this be your wish-fulfilling source."
Mutual nourishment between humans,
nature,
and the sacred order
keeps the world aligned.
Those who take
without returning
are thieves of the common good.
Those who share and offer
are released from stain.
The wise do not act
because they lack anything.
They act
because the world learns by example.
If leaders withdraw into indifference,
social order decays.
So act vigorously,
without ego-claim,
for the welfare of all.

title: The Measure of True Offering
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Some offer material gifts.
Some offer discipline.
Some offer study.
Some offer breath itself,
balancing inhale and exhale
as sacred exchange.
All sincere offerings purify.
Yet knowledge-offering exceeds all,
because every action
culminates in understanding.
Seek those who see truth.
Ask with humility.
Serve.
Question clearly.
They will transmit what frees.
Knowing this,
you will not fall again into delusion.
You will see all beings
in your own Self,
and your Self in Me.
Even if your past is heavy,
knowledge is a crossing.
Even if karma is thick,
knowledge is a fire.
Even if doubt is sharp,
knowledge is a sword.
Nothing in this world
purifies like direct insight.
In time,
one perfected in yoga
finds it within.
The doubting self is split,
without footing in this world
or the next,
without peace.
Therefore cut doubt
with insight born of yoga.
Take your stand.
Act.

title: Friend and Enemy Within
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Lift yourself by yourself.
Do not degrade yourself.
Mind can be your friend,
or your saboteur.
For one who has mastered mind,
mind is ally.
For one unmastered,
mind behaves as enemy.
The self-governed remain steady
in heat and cold,
honor and insult,
pleasure and pain.
Seated in discipline,
content in knowledge and direct experience,
they see equally
a clod,
a stone,
and gold.
They hold equal regard
for friend and foe,
for neutral and hostile,
for saint and sinner.
Choose a clean, quiet place.
Seat firm,
neither too high nor too low.
Body upright,
attention focused,
fear calmed,
energy regulated.
Practice with determination.
When the restless mind wanders,
bring it back.
Again.
Again.
No drama.
Just return.
From this training comes
supreme quiet,
where joy is discovered inwardly,
and sorrow cannot uproot you.
That union,
once attained,
is not traded for anything else.
Yes, the mind is restless,
strong,
hard to restrain.
But with practice and non-attachment,
it can be mastered.
Then yoga becomes lived reality.

title: Many Seek, Few Stay
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Among thousands,
one strives for realization.
Among those who strive,
rarely one knows Me as I am.
Still,
I receive every sincere approach.
Four kinds turn toward Me:
the distressed,
the seeker of benefit,
the seeker of truth,
and the one of wisdom.
All are noble.
The one rooted in wisdom,
whose love is single-hearted,
is especially dear.
Why?
Because that one does not treat Me
as an emergency exit
or transaction partner.
That one lives in union.
I am dear to them,
and they are dear to Me.
After many lifetimes,
a great soul arrives at this:
"All this is the One."
Such a being is very rare.
Desires pull minds toward lesser powers.
Faith is granted according to that turning,
and results are gained,
but they are temporary.
Those who seek finite fruits
arrive at finite ends.
Those who seek Me
arrive in Me.
I am not revealed
to minds veiled by delusion.
Past,
present,
future—I know all beings.
Few truly know Me.
Cross beyond duality,
and clarity opens.

title: The Practice of Remembering in Battle
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Remember Me at all times—
and fight.
This is not contradiction.
It is integration.
Mind and action aligned.
Whatever state one remembers
at the final moment,
that state is reached.
Therefore train now,
not at the edge of breath.
Build remembrance in motion.
With mind disciplined by practice,
not wandering to other tracks,
one reaches the Supreme Person.
Luminous,
ancient,
subtle,
sustainer of all,
beyond darkness.
At departure,
with unwavering mind,
life-force gathered between the brows,
one reaches the Divine.
This is the path of yogic exit,
prepared through lifelong alignment.
The Absolute declared by sages,
entered by disciplined seekers,
attained by those who walk celibate concentration—I summarize for you.
Fix awareness.
Steady breath.
Remember.
Yet know this too:
all realms,
from highest heaven down,
cycle through return.
The one who reaches Me
does not return to sorrow-bound rebirth.
So in every hour:
remember,
offer,
act.
Make life a preparation for freedom.

title: Love Makes the Vast Intimate
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: I am equal to all beings.
No one is hated,
no one is preferred.
Yet those who love Me deeply
live in Me,
and I in them.
Love reveals reciprocity.
Even one with a severe past,
if wholly devoted,
should be regarded as righteous in resolve.
Soon that person becomes aligned
and finds lasting peace.
Know this firmly:
My devotee is never lost.
Birth,
status,
and social label do not block access.
Anyone who takes refuge
can reach the highest state.
How much more those
already trained in discipline.
This world is unstable and passing.
Turn toward what endures.
Fix mind on Me.
Be devoted.
Offer your acts.
Bow in humility.
United with Me,
self-disciplined,
you will come to Me.
The path is not elitist.
It is relational.
A leaf offered with love
is greater than a palace offered in pride.
When the heart is total,
the Infinite is near.

title: After the Vision, the Choice
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: You have heard the deepest teaching:
knowledge,
action,
devotion,
renunciation,
clarity of duty,
and the way beyond sorrow.
Now examine it fully,
and act as you choose.
Is this permission to drift?
No.
It is respect for awakened agency.
Compulsion ends
where understanding begins.
If ego says,
"I will not act,"
your own nature will still drive you.
Bound by your constitutive force,
you will do what you resist.
Better to align consciously
than be dragged unconsciously.
The Lord dwells in the heart of all,
turning beings through the machinery of nature.
Take refuge in that indwelling source.
Through grace,
you will gain supreme peace.
Most secret of all:
you are dear.
So I speak plainly.
Fix your mind on Me.
Love Me.
Offer to Me.
Bow to Me.
You will come to Me.
This is promise.
Abandon all lesser shelters.
Take refuge in Me alone.
I free you from all bondage.
Do not fear.
Arjuna answers:
"My confusion is shattered.
Memory restored.
I stand firm.
I will do what must be done."

title: The Listener's Benediction
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Sanjaya says:
By grace,
I heard this supreme,
secret dialogue
between the Lord of Yoga
and the great-souled archer.
Again and again I recall it,
and again I rejoice.
Again I remember the cosmic form,
and wonder floods me.
Wherever Krishna,
master of yoga,
stands,
and wherever Arjuna,
master of action,
stands—
there abide fortune,
victory,
prosperity,
and firm justice.
This is my conviction.
Not sentiment,
not rumor.
A law written in the structure of reality:
wisdom joined to courageous action
is unstoppable.

Remember while your hands are working.

to practice remembrance.
Remember while your hands are working.
excerpt: Do not wait for death to practice remembrance.

title: One Moment, One Direction
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Final thought is trajectory.
Train now.
stanzas: Final thought is trajectory.
Train now.
excerpt: Final thought is trajectory. Train now.

title: Dawn Gate and Dusk Gate
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Some paths return.
Some do not.
Consciousness decides.
stanzas: Some paths return.
Some do not.
Consciousness decides.
excerpt: Some paths return. Some do not.

title: Hidden in Human Form
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: The Infinite can wear ordinary skin—
most eyes
miss it.
stanzas: The Infinite can wear ordinary skin—
most eyes
miss it.
excerpt: The Infinite can wear ordinary skin— most eyes

title: Even Misdirected Worship Reaches
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: All sincere prayer travels—
though not all of it
arrives at the Highest.
stanzas: All sincere prayer travels—
though not all of it
arrives at the Highest.
excerpt: All sincere prayer travels— though not all of it

title: Finite Gods, Finite Fruits
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: What you aim at
sets your ceiling.
stanzas: What you aim at
sets your ceiling.
excerpt: What you aim at sets your ceiling.

title: Undisturbed and Undisturbing
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Harm none.
Be rattled by none.
That is quiet power.
stanzas: Harm none.
Be rattled by none.
That is quiet power.
excerpt: Harm none. Be rattled by none.

title: Field Manual for Humility
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Humility,
nonviolence,
clean intent—knowledge begins there.
stanzas: Humility,
nonviolence,
clean intent—knowledge begins there.
excerpt: Humility, nonviolence,

title: Three Gates to Ruin
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Craving.
Anger.
Greed—close all three.
stanzas: Craving.
Anger.
Greed—close all three.
excerpt: Craving. Anger.

title: As Is Faith, So Is the Life
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: You become
what you bow to
repeatedly.
stanzas: You become
what you bow to
repeatedly.
excerpt: You become what you bow to

title: Smoke in Every Fire
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Every duty carries flaws—
do it anyway,
cleanly.
stanzas: Every duty carries flaws—
do it anyway,
cleanly.
excerpt: Every duty carries flaws— do it anyway,

title: Siege Horns
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: They blew the conches,
and the earth answered.
Fear moved through both armies.
stanzas: They blew the conches,
and the earth answered.
Fear moved through both armies.
excerpt: They blew the conches, and the earth answered.

title: Crisis at Kurukshetra
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Two armies faced each other,
but the real war opened
inside one man.
stanzas: Two armies faced each other,
but the real war opened
inside one man.
excerpt: Two armies faced each other, but the real war opened

title: Order of Battle
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Princes, teachers, cousins, kings—
all arranged in perfect lines
for imperfect ends.
Pride counted heroes by name,
as if naming could cancel mortality.
Steel flashed,
horses stamped,
drums became weather.
No one was innocent.
No one was outside history.
The field held everyone accountable.
stanzas: Princes, teachers, cousins, kings—
all arranged in perfect lines
for imperfect ends.
Pride counted heroes by name,
as if naming could cancel mortality.
Steel flashed,
horses stamped,
drums became weather.
No one was innocent.
No one was outside history.
The field held everyone accountable.
excerpt: Princes, teachers, cousins, kings— all arranged in perfect lines

title: Request to the Charioteer
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Drive me forward,
into the space between certainties.
I need to see who I am about to fight.
Not faceless enemies—
faces.
My own bloodline split into banners.
Truth begins when distance ends.
He asked to look closely,
and that request broke him open.
stanzas: Drive me forward,
into the space between certainties.
I need to see who I am about to fight.
Not faceless enemies—
faces.
My own bloodline split into banners.
Truth begins when distance ends.
He asked to look closely,
and that request broke him open.
excerpt: Drive me forward, into the space between certainties.

title: Anatomy of Collapse
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Mouth dry.
Skin burning.
Bow slipping from the hand.
This is how moral shock enters the body—
not as theory,
but as trembling.
He saw teachers and grandfathers
standing where targets should be,
and every war slogan went mute.
Sometimes compassion arrives late,
right before irreversible action.
stanzas: Mouth dry.
Skin burning.
Bow slipping from the hand.
This is how moral shock enters the body—
not as theory,
but as trembling.
He saw teachers and grandfathers
standing where targets should be,
and every war slogan went mute.
Sometimes compassion arrives late,
right before irreversible action.
excerpt: Mouth dry. Skin burning.

title: The Price of Victory
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: What is a kingdom
if those who would celebrate it are gone?
What is pleasure
when the table is empty?
He measured success against grief
and found the math obscene.
A throne can be won
and still feel like failure.
He chose the harder question:
what kind of victory
does not poison the winner?
stanzas: What is a kingdom
if those who would celebrate it are gone?
What is pleasure
when the table is empty?
He measured success against grief
and found the math obscene.
A throne can be won
and still feel like failure.
He chose the harder question:
what kind of victory
does not poison the winner?
excerpt: What is a kingdom if those who would celebrate it are gone?

title: Refusal at Full Draw
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: He could have fought.
Skill was not the issue.
Will was.
He dropped his weapons
in front of both armies,
and that silence was louder than horns.
To refuse violence
in the final second—
that is also a kind of courage.
But refusal is not always resolution.
It can be the doorway to deeper duty.
stanzas: He could have fought.
Skill was not the issue.
Will was.
He dropped his weapons
in front of both armies,
and that silence was louder than horns.
To refuse violence
in the final second—
that is also a kind of courage.
But refusal is not always resolution.
It can be the doorway to deeper duty.
excerpt: He could have fought. Skill was not the issue.

title: Rebuke Against Weakness
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Do not confuse nobility with collapse.
Do not name paralysis "compassion."
This moment asks for clarity,
not theatrical despair.
Stand up.
The crisis inside you
is not your final form.
The first instruction was not mystical—
it was tactical:
rise.
stanzas: Do not confuse nobility with collapse.
Do not name paralysis "compassion."
This moment asks for clarity,
not theatrical despair.
Stand up.
The crisis inside you
is not your final form.
The first instruction was not mystical—
it was tactical:
rise.
excerpt: Do not confuse nobility with collapse. Do not name paralysis "compassion."

title: Endurance Doctrine
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Sensation is weather.
Heat, cold, pleasure, pain—
all of it passes.
If you build identity
from passing conditions,
you fracture with every season.
Learn to stay.
Not numb,
steady.
Endurance is not denial.
It is refusing to be ruled by fluctuation.
stanzas: Sensation is weather.
Heat, cold, pleasure, pain—
all of it passes.
If you build identity
from passing conditions,
you fracture with every season.
Learn to stay.
Not numb,
steady.
Endurance is not denial.
It is refusing to be ruled by fluctuation.
excerpt: Sensation is weather. Heat, cold, pleasure, pain—

title: Duty and Reputation
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: You are not free
from the consequences of inaction.
If you abandon rightful duty,
you lose more than battle—
you lose your moral position.
People remember cowardice longer than defeat.
For one trusted with arms,
retreat from justice
is its own violence.
stanzas: You are not free
from the consequences of inaction.
If you abandon rightful duty,
you lose more than battle—
you lose your moral position.
People remember cowardice longer than defeat.
For one trusted with arms,
retreat from justice
is its own violence.
excerpt: You are not free from the consequences of inaction.

title: One Mind or Many Branches
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: A disciplined intellect is singular.
A desire-driven intellect
splinters into endless agendas.
One seeks truth.
The other seeks decorated distraction.
When attention fragments,
life fragments with it.
Gather your forces.
War is won
before contact.
stanzas: A disciplined intellect is singular.
A desire-driven intellect
splinters into endless agendas.
One seeks truth.
The other seeks decorated distraction.
When attention fragments,
life fragments with it.
Gather your forces.
War is won
before contact.
excerpt: A disciplined intellect is singular. A desire-driven intellect

title: The Market of Heavenly Promises
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: There are merchants of religion
who sell bright afterlives
for present obedience.
They promise pleasure,
status,
celestial upgrades.
But desire in sacred language
is still desire.
Step beyond reward-based faith.
Look for what cannot be bought,
won,
or advertised.
stanzas: There are merchants of religion
who sell bright afterlives
for present obedience.
They promise pleasure,
status,
celestial upgrades.
But desire in sacred language
is still desire.
Step beyond reward-based faith.
Look for what cannot be bought,
won,
or advertised.
excerpt: There are merchants of religion who sell bright afterlives

title: From Reaction to Ruin
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Attention sticks.
Attachment forms.
Demand rises.
Demand blocked—anger.
Anger—distortion.
Distortion—memory failure.
When memory fails,
judgment falls.
When judgment falls,
self-destruction is close.
Catch the chain early,
or it will finish itself.
stanzas: Attention sticks.
Attachment forms.
Demand rises.
Demand blocked—anger.
Anger—distortion.
Distortion—memory failure.
When memory fails,
judgment falls.
When judgment falls,
self-destruction is close.
Catch the chain early,
or it will finish itself.
excerpt: Attention sticks. Attachment forms.

title: Public Duty of the Wise
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: The wise do not need anything,
yet they still act.
Not for personal gain—
for social coherence.
If those with vision withdraw,
confusion scales.
If they engage,
order holds.
Private enlightenment
without public responsibility
is incomplete.
stanzas: The wise do not need anything,
yet they still act.
Not for personal gain—
for social coherence.
If those with vision withdraw,
confusion scales.
If they engage,
order holds.
Private enlightenment
without public responsibility
is incomplete.
excerpt: The wise do not need anything, yet they still act.

title: Ritual as Engine
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Offering is not superstition.
It is reciprocity built into reality.
Give, and systems breathe.
Take only, and systems starve.
Rain, food, life, labor—
a chain of mutual debt.
To honor the chain is sacred.
To exploit it is collapse.
stanzas: Offering is not superstition.
It is reciprocity built into reality.
Give, and systems breathe.
Take only, and systems starve.
Rain, food, life, labor—
a chain of mutual debt.
To honor the chain is sacred.
To exploit it is collapse.
excerpt: Offering is not superstition. It is reciprocity built into reality.

title: Inner Enemy Briefing
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Name the adversary: craving.
Its camouflage: pleasure.
Its appetite: endless.
It occupies senses,
mind,
and intellect.
Secure the perimeter.
Discipline the channels.
Then anchor in what is higher than mind.
Only that command level
can end the siege.
stanzas: Name the adversary: craving.
Its camouflage: pleasure.
Its appetite: endless.
It occupies senses,
mind,
and intellect.
Secure the perimeter.
Discipline the channels.
Then anchor in what is higher than mind.
Only that command level
can end the siege.
excerpt: Name the adversary: craving. Its camouflage: pleasure.

title: Transmission Lineage
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: This teaching moved through generations
until time blurred it.
Now it is spoken again
on a battlefield,
not in a monastery.
Truth survives by transmission,
not preservation alone.
Each era must re-hear it
in its own language of urgency.
stanzas: This teaching moved through generations
until time blurred it.
Now it is spoken again
on a battlefield,
not in a monastery.
Truth survives by transmission,
not preservation alone.
Each era must re-hear it
in its own language of urgency.
excerpt: This teaching moved through generations until time blurred it.

title: Divine Descent Protocol
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: When justice thins,
when predation rises,
intervention enters history.
Not as spectacle—
as correction.
Protection of the vulnerable.
Containment of the violent.
Restoration of law.
The sacred does not watch passively.
It deploys.
stanzas: When justice thins,
when predation rises,
intervention enters history.
Not as spectacle—
as correction.
Protection of the vulnerable.
Containment of the violent.
Restoration of law.
The sacred does not watch passively.
It deploys.
excerpt: When justice thins, when predation rises,

title: Action Seen Correctly
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Sometimes motion is bondage.
Sometimes stillness is avoidance.
Wisdom reads the hidden motive,
not the visible gesture.
One can act intensely
without ego ownership.
One can retire outwardly
while craving inwardly.
See the engine,
not just the form.
stanzas: Sometimes motion is bondage.
Sometimes stillness is avoidance.
Wisdom reads the hidden motive,
not the visible gesture.
One can act intensely
without ego ownership.
One can retire outwardly
while craving inwardly.
See the engine,
not just the form.
excerpt: Sometimes motion is bondage. Sometimes stillness is avoidance.

title: Sacrifices of Many Kinds
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Some offer resources.
Some offer discipline.
Some offer breath.
Some offer study.
Each valid,
if sincere.
But the offering of understanding
turns the entire life into altar fire.
When ignorance burns,
action burns clean.
stanzas: Some offer resources.
Some offer discipline.
Some offer breath.
Some offer study.
Each valid,
if sincere.
But the offering of understanding
turns the entire life into altar fire.
When ignorance burns,
action burns clean.
excerpt: Some offer resources. Some offer discipline.

title: Posture of Meditation
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Choose a clean place.
Sit stable.
Spine aligned.
Gaze softened.
Regulate food,
sleep,
speech,
effort.
When mind bolts,
return it—firmly, repeatedly.
Practice is not a single breakthrough.
It is a thousand returns.
stanzas: Choose a clean place.
Sit stable.
Spine aligned.
Gaze softened.
Regulate food,
sleep,
speech,
effort.
When mind bolts,
return it—firmly, repeatedly.
Practice is not a single breakthrough.
It is a thousand returns.
excerpt: Choose a clean place. Sit stable.

title: Yoga Interrupted
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: What if one starts,
then falls away?
No sincere movement is erased.
Momentum carries forward,
even across death.
Conditions return,
doorways reopen,
practice resumes.
In this work,
setbacks are pauses,
not verdicts.
stanzas: What if one starts,
then falls away?
No sincere movement is erased.
Momentum carries forward,
even across death.
Conditions return,
doorways reopen,
practice resumes.
In this work,
setbacks are pauses,
not verdicts.
excerpt: What if one starts, then falls away?

title: Field and Knower Expanded
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Body is field.
Awareness is knower.
Confusion comes when they are fused.
The field changes:
age,
illness,
pleasure,
pain.
The knower witnesses change
without becoming change.
Learn that distinction,
and identity stops leaking into circumstance.
stanzas: Body is field.
Awareness is knower.
Confusion comes when they are fused.
The field changes:
age,
illness,
pleasure,
pain.
The knower witnesses change
without becoming change.
Learn that distinction,
and identity stops leaking into circumstance.
excerpt: Body is field. Awareness is knower.

title: Qualities in Rotation
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Clarity lifts.
Passion drives.
Inertia drags.
These three rotate through everyone.
Do not over-identify with any phase.
Observe.
Adjust.
Rise.
Freedom begins
when you stop saying:
"This mode is me."
stanzas: Clarity lifts.
Passion drives.
Inertia drags.
These three rotate through everyone.
Do not over-identify with any phase.
Observe.
Adjust.
Rise.
Freedom begins
when you stop saying:
"This mode is me."
excerpt: Clarity lifts. Passion drives.

title: Tree with Roots Above
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: A world-tree grows downward,
fed by desire and habit.
Its branches are endless outcomes.
Its true root is above,
outside the market of becoming.
Cut attachment with disciplined detachment.
Then trace the root back
to the source beyond return.
Most study the branches.
Few seek origin.
stanzas: A world-tree grows downward,
fed by desire and habit.
Its branches are endless outcomes.
Its true root is above,
outside the market of becoming.
Cut attachment with disciplined detachment.
Then trace the root back
to the source beyond return.
Most study the branches.
Few seek origin.
excerpt: A world-tree grows downward, fed by desire and habit.

title: Divine and Undivine Profiles
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Fearlessness,
truth,
self-control,
compassion—these liberate.
Arrogance,
harshness,
greed,
self-worship—these bind.
Character is strategy.
You are always training one pattern
or the other.
Choose what kind of power
you become.
stanzas: Fearlessness,
truth,
self-control,
compassion—these liberate.
Arrogance,
harshness,
greed,
self-worship—these bind.
Character is strategy.
You are always training one pattern
or the other.
Choose what kind of power
you become.
excerpt: Fearlessness, truth,

title: Architecture of Food and Faith
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: What you eat,
what you praise,
what you practice in private—
all shape consciousness.
Clarity seeks what sustains.
Restlessness seeks stimulation.
Inertia seeks decay.
Diet is not only physical.
Attention is also intake.
Consume with intention,
or your mind will mirror your cravings.
stanzas: What you eat,
what you praise,
what you practice in private—
all shape consciousness.
Clarity seeks what sustains.
Restlessness seeks stimulation.
Inertia seeks decay.
Diet is not only physical.
Attention is also intake.
Consume with intention,
or your mind will mirror your cravings.
excerpt: What you eat, what you praise,

title: Threefold Gift
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Give at the right time,
right place,
to real need,
without demand—this purifies.
Give for applause,
and the gift shrinks.
Give carelessly,
and both giver and receiver are harmed.
Generosity is measured
by intention,
not size.
A clean gift
is silent and exact.
stanzas: Give at the right time,
right place,
to real need,
without demand—this purifies.
Give for applause,
and the gift shrinks.
Give carelessly,
and both giver and receiver are harmed.
Generosity is measured
by intention,
not size.
A clean gift
is silent and exact.
excerpt: Give at the right time, right place,

title: Threefold Austerity
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Body discipline:
clean conduct,
respect,
non-harm.
Speech discipline:
truth,
kindness,
utility.
Mind discipline:
calm,
sincerity,
self-command.
Without ego display,
austerity clarifies.
With vanity,
it corrodes.
stanzas: Body discipline:
clean conduct,
respect,
non-harm.
Speech discipline:
truth,
kindness,
utility.
Mind discipline:
calm,
sincerity,
self-command.
Without ego display,
austerity clarifies.
With vanity,
it corrodes.
excerpt: Body discipline: clean conduct,

title: Renunciation Clarified
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Do not abandon necessary action.
Abandon ownership of results.
That is the core distinction.
Escaping duty in confusion
is inertia dressed as spirituality.
Act where you are placed.
Release the claim.
This is mature renunciation.
stanzas: Do not abandon necessary action.
Abandon ownership of results.
That is the core distinction.
Escaping duty in confusion
is inertia dressed as spirituality.
Act where you are placed.
Release the claim.
This is mature renunciation.
excerpt: Do not abandon necessary action. Abandon ownership of results.

title: Knowledge, Action, Doer
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Knowledge differs by lens.
Action differs by motive.
Doer differs by inner state.
The same outward deed
can bind one person
and free another.
What matters is quality of consciousness
at the point of execution.
Refine the doer,
and action refines itself.
stanzas: Knowledge differs by lens.
Action differs by motive.
Doer differs by inner state.
The same outward deed
can bind one person
and free another.
What matters is quality of consciousness
at the point of execution.
Refine the doer,
and action refines itself.
excerpt: Knowledge differs by lens. Action differs by motive.

title: Intellect and Resolve
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: One intellect distinguishes right from wrong.
Another rationalizes appetite.
A third cannot decide at all.
Resolve follows the same pattern:
steady,
ambitious,
or dull.
Train discernment first.
Willpower without discernment
amplifies mistakes.
stanzas: One intellect distinguishes right from wrong.
Another rationalizes appetite.
A third cannot decide at all.
Resolve follows the same pattern:
steady,
ambitious,
or dull.
Train discernment first.
Willpower without discernment
amplifies mistakes.
excerpt: One intellect distinguishes right from wrong. Another rationalizes appetite.

title: Happiness by Type
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Some joy tastes bitter first,
then becomes nectar.
Some starts sweet,
then hollows the heart.
Some numbs from the start.
Learn to read trajectories,
not first impressions.
The best happiness
builds strength after it passes.
stanzas: Some joy tastes bitter first,
then becomes nectar.
Some starts sweet,
then hollows the heart.
Some numbs from the start.
Learn to read trajectories,
not first impressions.
The best happiness
builds strength after it passes.
excerpt: Some joy tastes bitter first, then becomes nectar.

title: Better One's Own Work
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Your own duty,
imperfectly lived,
beats borrowed brilliance.
Imitation breeds anxiety.
Alignment builds force.
Every role has friction.
Smoke follows fire.
Stay with the work
that is yours to carry.
stanzas: Your own duty,
imperfectly lived,
beats borrowed brilliance.
Imitation breeds anxiety.
Alignment builds force.
Every role has friction.
Smoke follows fire.
Stay with the work
that is yours to carry.
excerpt: Your own duty, imperfectly lived,

title: From Hearing to Decision
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Instruction was given.
Doubt was examined.
Vision was widened.
Then came the final respect:
consider fully,
and choose.
Spiritual authority does not erase agency.
It sharpens it.
Clarity is complete
when responsibility returns to you.
stanzas: Instruction was given.
Doubt was examined.
Vision was widened.
Then came the final respect:
consider fully,
and choose.
Spiritual authority does not erase agency.
It sharpens it.
Clarity is complete
when responsibility returns to you.
excerpt: Instruction was given. Doubt was examined.

title: Sanjaya's Witness
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: I heard the exchange
between disciplined action
and divine intelligence.
Every recollection returns as power.
Every recollection returns as awe.
Where wisdom and courage align,
fortune follows as consequence,
not luck.
That is my final report.
stanzas: I heard the exchange
between disciplined action
and divine intelligence.
Every recollection returns as power.
Every recollection returns as awe.
Where wisdom and courage align,
fortune follows as consequence,
not luck.
That is my final report.
excerpt: I heard the exchange between disciplined action

title: Before Choice, There Is Seeing
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: He asked to see the lines clearly,
friend and foe arranged in one frame.
No slogans.
No safe distance.
He saw elders,
teachers,
cousins,
children of shared houses.
The battle map turned human.
Ethics begins at this threshold:
when abstractions dissolve into faces.
Only then can duty be chosen
without self-deception.
stanzas: He asked to see the lines clearly,
friend and foe arranged in one frame.
No slogans.
No safe distance.
He saw elders,
teachers,
cousins,
children of shared houses.
The battle map turned human.
Ethics begins at this threshold:
when abstractions dissolve into faces.
Only then can duty be chosen
without self-deception.
excerpt: He asked to see the lines clearly, friend and foe arranged in one frame.

title: The Battlefield as Classroom
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: The teaching was not delivered
in retreat,
but between drawn armies.
Because real wisdom
must survive pressure,
noise,
and irreversible stakes.
The setting matters:
truth that cannot function in conflict
is not yet integrated.
What holds in battle
will hold in life.
stanzas: The teaching was not delivered
in retreat,
but between drawn armies.
Because real wisdom
must survive pressure,
noise,
and irreversible stakes.
The setting matters:
truth that cannot function in conflict
is not yet integrated.
What holds in battle
will hold in life.
excerpt: The teaching was not delivered in retreat,

title: Moral Injury, Named Early
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: He predicted the aftershock:
if we kill kin,
the social fabric tears.
Ritual memory fades.
Family law dissolves.
Vulnerability multiplies.
He was not sentimental.
He was systemic.
Violence does not end
when the weapons stop.
It enters generations.
stanzas: He predicted the aftershock:
if we kill kin,
the social fabric tears.
Ritual memory fades.
Family law dissolves.
Vulnerability multiplies.
He was not sentimental.
He was systemic.
Violence does not end
when the weapons stop.
It enters generations.
excerpt: He predicted the aftershock: if we kill kin,

title: Instruction to Rise
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: The first correction was embodied:
stand.
Not because all doubt is gone,
but because collapse
cannot host discernment.
Then came philosophy,
metaphysics,
strategy,
devotion.
But none of it lands
until posture changes.
stanzas: The first correction was embodied:
stand.
Not because all doubt is gone,
but because collapse
cannot host discernment.
Then came philosophy,
metaphysics,
strategy,
devotion.
But none of it lands
until posture changes.
excerpt: The first correction was embodied: stand.

title: Transience as Training Ground
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Everything sensed is temporary,
which is not a reason for despair.
It is a reason for discipline.
If experiences are passing,
let them pass without ownership.
If moods are passing,
stop building identity from weather.
Impermanence is not nihilism—
it is tactical clarity.
stanzas: Everything sensed is temporary,
which is not a reason for despair.
It is a reason for discipline.
If experiences are passing,
let them pass without ownership.
If moods are passing,
stop building identity from weather.
Impermanence is not nihilism—
it is tactical clarity.
excerpt: Everything sensed is temporary, which is not a reason for despair.

title: Doctrine of Necessary Action
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: No one can opt out of action.
Breathing acts.
Thinking acts.
Refusing acts.
The question is never
"Will I act?"
It is:
"From what center?"
Act from appetite,
and you bind yourself.
Act from offering,
and action becomes clean.
The mechanics are unavoidable.
The motive is the battlefield.
stanzas: No one can opt out of action.
Breathing acts.
Thinking acts.
Refusing acts.
The question is never
"Will I act?"
It is:
"From what center?"
Act from appetite,
and you bind yourself.
Act from offering,
and action becomes clean.
The mechanics are unavoidable.
The motive is the battlefield.
excerpt: No one can opt out of action. Breathing acts.

title: Public Consequence of Private Confusion
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: When leaders normalize confusion,
cultures inherit it.
When they normalize integrity,
cultures steady.
Private motives do not stay private.
They become policy,
custom,
future.
That is why the wise keep acting,
even without personal need.
Responsibility scales.
stanzas: When leaders normalize confusion,
cultures inherit it.
When they normalize integrity,
cultures steady.
Private motives do not stay private.
They become policy,
custom,
future.
That is why the wise keep acting,
even without personal need.
Responsibility scales.
excerpt: When leaders normalize confusion, cultures inherit it.

title: Manual for Mastering Desire
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Desire enters through contact,
settles in preference,
and hardens into demand.
When blocked,
it ignites anger.
When fed,
it wants more.
Do not negotiate from inside the fire.
Secure the senses first.
Then steady the mind,
clarify the intellect,
and root identity deeper than both.
Only from that depth
can craving be commanded.
stanzas: Desire enters through contact,
settles in preference,
and hardens into demand.
When blocked,
it ignites anger.
When fed,
it wants more.
Do not negotiate from inside the fire.
Secure the senses first.
Then steady the mind,
clarify the intellect,
and root identity deeper than both.
Only from that depth
can craving be commanded.
excerpt: Desire enters through contact, settles in preference,

title: How to Approach a Teacher
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Not with flattery.
Not with argument theater.
Approach with humility,
service,
and exact questions.
A true teacher does not give you beliefs.
They give you instruments.
Then insight becomes self-verifying,
not borrowed.
Transmission is earned
through sincerity.
stanzas: Not with flattery.
Not with argument theater.
Approach with humility,
service,
and exact questions.
A true teacher does not give you beliefs.
They give you instruments.
Then insight becomes self-verifying,
not borrowed.
Transmission is earned
through sincerity.
excerpt: Not with flattery. Not with argument theater.

title: Meditation as Repeated Return
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Prepare the body.
Regulate habits.
Simplify environment.
Then sit,
and watch the mind run.
Each time it runs,
bring it back.
No self-hatred.
No drama.
This is the hidden heroism:
ordinary repetition
until attention obeys.
Steadiness is manufactured,
not granted.
stanzas: Prepare the body.
Regulate habits.
Simplify environment.
Then sit,
and watch the mind run.
Each time it runs,
bring it back.
No self-hatred.
No drama.
This is the hidden heroism:
ordinary repetition
until attention obeys.
Steadiness is manufactured,
not granted.
excerpt: Prepare the body. Regulate habits.

title: On Incomplete Practice
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: What if effort breaks midstream?
What if life interrupts?
No clean attempt is wasted.
The trajectory persists
beneath visible failure.
Conditions reassemble.
Practice returns.
Progress in this discipline
is cumulative beyond one chapter,
beyond one lifetime.
Trust the long arc,
then continue.
stanzas: What if effort breaks midstream?
What if life interrupts?
No clean attempt is wasted.
The trajectory persists
beneath visible failure.
Conditions reassemble.
Practice returns.
Progress in this discipline
is cumulative beyond one chapter,
beyond one lifetime.
Trust the long arc,
then continue.
excerpt: What if effort breaks midstream? What if life interrupts?

title: Structure of Reality, Briefed
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Matter is one layer:
elements,
mind,
intellect,
ego-structure.
Conscious life is another layer:
the animating principle,
subtler,
more decisive.
Confusion begins
when the lower layer claims totality.
Discern both.
Then relation to the world shifts
from possession to participation.
stanzas: Matter is one layer:
elements,
mind,
intellect,
ego-structure.
Conscious life is another layer:
the animating principle,
subtler,
more decisive.
Confusion begins
when the lower layer claims totality.
Discern both.
Then relation to the world shifts
from possession to participation.
excerpt: Matter is one layer: elements,

title: Training for Final Memory
author: Bhagavad Gita
lines: Final thought is not random.
It is trained by repetition.
So remember the Highest
in labor,
conversation,
conflict,
silence.
At death,
you will not invent a new center.
You will return to your practiced one.
Train now,
when choice is wide.
stanzas: Final thought is not random.
It is trained by repetition.
So remember the Highest
in labor,
conversation,
conflict,
silence.
At death,
you will not invent a new center.
You will return to your practiced one.
Train now,
when choice is wide.

title: Chasing the Wind
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: Smoke of smoke—everything is smoke.
What does a person gain
from all the labor
under the sun?
stanzas: Smoke of smoke—everything is smoke.
What does a person gain
from all the labor
under the sun?
excerpt: Smoke of smoke—everything is smoke. What does a person gain

title: The Returning Circuit
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: The sun rises.
The sun goes down.
It runs back to where it began.
stanzas: The sun rises.
The sun goes down.
It runs back to where it began.
excerpt: The sun rises. The sun goes down.

title: Never Filled
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: All rivers run to the sea,
yet the sea is never full.
The waters keep returning
by the same old paths.
stanzas: All rivers run to the sea,
yet the sea is never full.
The waters keep returning
by the same old paths.
excerpt: All rivers run to the sea, yet the sea is never full.

title: A Crooked Thing
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: What is crooked
cannot be straightened.
What is missing
cannot be counted.
stanzas: What is crooked
cannot be straightened.
What is missing
cannot be counted.
excerpt: What is crooked cannot be straightened.

title: Many Days of Darkness
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: Rejoice in the light—yes.
But remember:
many days of darkness are coming.
stanzas: Rejoice in the light—yes.
But remember:
many days of darkness are coming.

title: Dust Returning
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: Dust returns to the earth
as it was.
Breath returns to God
who gave it.
stanzas: Dust returns to the earth
as it was.
Breath returns to God
who gave it.
excerpt: Dust returns to the earth as it was.

title: No Better Thing
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: There is nothing better
than to eat,
to drink,
and to find good in your labor.
stanzas: There is nothing better
than to eat,
to drink,
and to find good in your labor.
excerpt: There is nothing better than to eat,

title: The Whole Duty
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: Fear God.
Keep his commands.
This is the whole weight
of being human.
stanzas: Fear God.
Keep his commands.
This is the whole weight
of being human.
excerpt: Fear God. Keep his commands.

title: Born for Trouble
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: A human born of woman
is short-lived
and full of trouble.
stanzas: A human born of woman
is short-lived
and full of trouble.
excerpt: A human born of woman is short-lived

title: Sparks Upward
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: A person is born for trouble
as surely
as sparks fly upward.
stanzas: A person is born for trouble
as surely
as sparks fly upward.
excerpt: A person is born for trouble as surely

title: Skin and Bones
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: My bones cling to my skin.
My days race by.
No hope follows.
stanzas: My bones cling to my skin.
My days race by.
No hope follows.
excerpt: My bones cling to my skin. My days race by.

title: Leviathan Laughs
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: Can you pull Leviathan out
with a hook?
He laughs at spears
as if they were straw.
stanzas: Can you pull Leviathan out
with a hook?
He laughs at spears
as if they were straw.
excerpt: Can you pull Leviathan out with a hook?

title: Treasuries of Snow
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: Have you entered
the storehouses of snow?
Have you seen
the treasuries of hail?
stanzas: Have you entered
the storehouses of snow?
Have you seen
the treasuries of hail?
excerpt: Have you entered the storehouses of snow?

title: Depth Calls
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: Deep calls to deep
in the roar of your waters.
All your waves
break over me.
stanzas: Deep calls to deep
in the roar of your waters.
All your waves
break over me.
excerpt: Deep calls to deep in the roar of your waters.

title: Still Waters
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: He leads me
beside still waters.
He restores
my soul.
stanzas: He leads me
beside still waters.
He restores
my soul.
excerpt: He leads me beside still waters.

title: The Crushed Heart
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: The Lord is near
to the brokenhearted.
He saves
those crushed in spirit.
stanzas: The Lord is near
to the brokenhearted.
He saves
those crushed in spirit.

title: Number My Days
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: Teach us
to number our days,
so we may gain
a wise heart.
stanzas: Teach us
to number our days,
so we may gain

title: Wake Before Dawn
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: I rise before dawn
and cry for help.
I put my hope
in your word.
stanzas: I rise before dawn
and cry for help.
I put my hope
in your word.
excerpt: I rise before dawn and cry for help.

title: Fear of the Lord
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: The fear of the Lord
is the beginning of wisdom.
Knowledge begins
with reverence.
stanzas: The fear of the Lord
is the beginning of wisdom.
Knowledge begins
with reverence.
excerpt: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.

title: Guard the Heart
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: Guard your heart
with all vigilance,
for from it
flow the springs of life.
stanzas: Guard your heart
with all vigilance,
for from it
flow the springs of life.
excerpt: Guard your heart with all vigilance,

title: Iron Sharpens Iron
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: Iron sharpens iron.
One person
sharpens another.
stanzas: Iron sharpens iron.
One person
sharpens another.
excerpt: Iron sharpens iron. One person

title: Soft Answer
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: A soft answer
turns away wrath.
A harsh word
stirs anger.
stanzas: A soft answer
turns away wrath.
A harsh word

excerpt: A soft answer turns away wrath.

title: When Pride Walks In
author: Anonymous (Wisdom Literature / Modernized)
lines: Pride walks in first.
Ruin follows.
A haughty spirit
goes before a fall.
stanzas: Pride walks in first.
Ruin follows.
A haughty spirit
goes before a fall.
